title: Hampy Nanay - AI surgery recovery assistant
description: Tool to help patients recover from surgery by providing personalized care plans and reminders
longDescription: Built during StartupMed, a healthcare-focused hackathon, Hampy Nanay is an AI-powered assistant designed to support patients recovering from surgery. The app generates personalized care plans based on the type of surgery, patient health data, and recovery goals. It provides daily reminders for medication, physical therapy exercises, and follow-up appointments. The project won the Best Healthcare Solution award and has been adopted by several local clinics for pilot testing.
learned: Integrated third-party AI APIs into a production application
Implemented complex scheduling algorithms for spaced repetition
Managed real-time state updates with optimistic UI patterns
Worked with a team under tight time constraints
challenges: Rate limiting and cost optimization for API calls
Designing an intuitive UX for complex scheduling logic
Handling edge cases in AI-generated content

title: Bracelet Studio
description: Game where you can build your own bracelet
longDescription: Created for girly game jam, this game allows players to design and customize their own bracelets using a variety of beads.
learned: Designing a user-friendly UI for customization
challenges: Managing game state persistence across sessions

title: AnemiaGuard
description: Offline-first mobile app for anemia risk estimation using ML, computer vision, and clinical data.
longDescription: Developed during Frostbyte hackathon, AnemiaRecovery is a mobile application designed to help individuals at risk of anemia. The app uses machine learning models trained on clinical data and computer vision techniques to analyze images of the user's fingernails for signs of anemia. It provides personalized recommendations for dietary changes and supplements, along with an offline-first design to ensure accessibility in low-connectivity areas.
learned: Training and deploying ML models on mobile devices
Implementing computer vision algorithms for health diagnostics
Navigating healthcare data privacy and security considerations
challenges: Optimizing ML model performance on resource-constrained devices
Ensuring accurate and reliable diagnostics with limited data
Designing an intuitive UI for health-related applications
